mapping.a_element: the groupoid induced by translations of the tiling
mapping.b_element: the pattern groupoid of the projection point pattern

mapping.a_element: the hull of the tiling as a dynamical system
mapping.b_element: the dynamical systems obtained from projection point patterns

mapping.a_element: the scaled ordered K_0-group as the topological invariant of the tiling groupoid (gap labelling)
mapping.b_element: the cocycle cohomology of the pattern groupoid as the topological invariant

mapping.a_element: computation for one-dimensional tilings and their Cartesian products
mapping.b_element: calculation for canonical projection method tilings of codimension at most 3

brief_justification: Both build a groupoid from the translations of an aperiodic tiling/point pattern (its hull as a dynamical system) and extract a topological invariant of that groupoid; A uses the ordered K_0-group of the tiling C*-algebra for gap labelling while B uses the cocycle cohomology of the pattern groupoid, but the transferable machinery -- groupoid-based topological invariants of tilings computed on specific families -- is the same.
